notes: PREPARATION FOR THE MINOR Complete the following: MUSC 80C: History, Literature, and Technology of Electronic Music Plus one course from the following: MUSC 11A: Introduction to Western Classical Music MUSC 11B: Introduction to Jazz MUSC 11C: Popular Music in the United States MUSC 11D: Introduction to World Music MUSC 11E: Global Popular Music MUSIC THEORY REQUIREMENT Students must take the Theory Placement Exam in order to test into the appropriate western music theory course (MUSC 14 or MUSC 30A). If a student places into MUSC 30A via the exam, they will automatically satisfy the minor's theory requirement. Students who place into MUSC 14 must pass the course to satisfy the minor's theory requirement. Please visit the Music Department website to learn more about this minor.

UC Santa Cruz students may choose to complete one or more minors, provided they can finish all required coursework within their maximum enrollment terms and allowable credits. While a minor will appear on a student's official transcript, it will not be listed on their diploma. Pursuing a minor is a personal decision that should align with a student's educational and career goals. Students do not apply for minors when submitting their application to UC Santa Cruz. After enrolling, transfer students interested in pursuing a minor should contact the department offering the minor for guidance.
